title: Ceraia lanciloba
taxonomy: Ceraia lanciloba is a species of moth in the family Sphingidae. It belongs to the genus Ceraia, which is a part of the subfamily Sphinginae. The family Sphingidae is a group of moths commonly known as hawk moths or sphinx moths. The classification of Ceraia lanciloba is further supported by its unique characteristics and physical features. This species is often found in specific regions and habitats, contributing to its unique classification within the Sphingidae family.

intro: Ceraia lanciloba, also known as the lancilobed Ceraia, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been found to exhibit various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Ceraia lanciloba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ceraia lanciloba is often used in traditional medicine to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly consumed for their health-promoting effects.
